steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states arthropods showed larger differences on average than birds, but for every class — including birds — individual studies showed differences well above the arthropod average. The blank must draw a conclusion consistent with this overlap.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'the difference found in the study conducted by Matthew A. Wale and colleagues was likely larger than the average difference for studies of shore crabs included in the meta-analysis.': The text gives no information comparing the Wale study to other shore crab studies in the meta-analysis. ✗
  • Choice B — 'the differences that studies attribute to exposure to anthropogenic noise are likely to be more pronounced for birds than they are for arthropods.': Contradicts the passage, which says arthropods showed larger average differences. ✗
  • Choice C — 'some studies of birds found larger effects of exposure to anthropogenic noise than some studies of arthropods did.': Directly follows from the stated fact that bird studies reached differences well above the arthropod average. ✓
  • Choice D — 'the studies in the meta-analysis that examined arthropods were more likely than those that examined birds to specify whether the observed effects were detrimental.': Whether effects were labeled ‘detrimental’ is never mentioned in the passage. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
